reasoning: The market resolves YES if federal government current expenditures (FGEXPND) in Q4 2025 are at least $250 billion lower than in Q4 2024. Federal spending has been continuously increasing throughout 2025. As of mid-2025, Q2 expenditures were up roughly $475 billion year-over-year, and CBO data corroborated widespread nominal expenditure growth. While DOGE claims finding approximately $180 billion in long-term savings or efficiencies, actual enacted cuts (e.g. the $9.4 billion rescission request) pale in comparison to mandatory increases in spending from Social Security, Medicare, and debt interest. Given that aggregate spending is growing substantially over 2024 levels, the absolute value in Q4 2025 will almost certainly be higher—not lower—than Q4 2024, making a $250 billion decrease extremely unlikely.
